channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Did FBI Director Kash Patel use AI to rip off the Beastie Boys?
item.description: An analysis by NPR suggests that frames from an iconic music video were used to generate content for a tweet by the FBI director.
item.content:encoded:
An analysis by NPR suggests that frames from an iconic music video were used to generate content for a tweet by the FBI director.

(Image credit: Screenshots by Emily Bogle for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: ICE is giving local police big money to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: The federal government is offering local law enforcement incentives to join a program that gives their officers authority to make immigration arrests. Police leaders say the funds, which include money for salaries, equipment and vehicles, are enticing.
item.content:encoded:
The federal government is offering local law enforcement incentives to join a program that gives their officers authority to make immigration arrests. Police leaders say the funds, which include money for salaries, equipment and vehicles, are enticing.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Here's a look at the 36 races for governor this year
item.description: The political environment doesn't look good for Republicans right now, but the party could make gains in the many races for governor across the country this November.
item.content:encoded: The political environment doesn't look good for Republicans right now, but the party could make gains in the many races for governor across the country this November.
item.dc:creator: Acacia Squires

item.title: If you are struggling to make ends meet, NPR wants to hear from you
item.description: With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.
item.content:encoded:
With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: SCOTUS temporarily restores mifepristone access. And, Indiana, Ohio vote in primaries
item.description: The Supreme Court temporarily restored access to the abortion pill mifepristone. And, Indiana's primaries will test President Trump's influence as he tries to oust incumbent Republican senators.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court temporarily restored access to the abortion pill mifepristone. And, Indiana's primaries will test President Trump's influence as he tries to oust incumbent Republican senators.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: U.S. says the Iran ceasefire holds despite attacks in Strait of Hormuz and against UAE
item.description: So far, only two merchant ships are known to have passed a new U.S.-guarded route through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
So far, only two merchant ships are known to have passed a new U.S.-guarded route through the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: This flashy group of Portland mall walkers puts neon pep into their step
item.description: Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.
item.content:encoded:
Krista Catwood wanted a fun way to exercise, so she started a mall-walking group whose unofficial costume was 1980s garb. The festive, all-ages event has exploded in popularity over the past year.

(Image credit: Celeste Noche for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Minneapolis grapples with the impact of Trump's largest immigration crackdown yet
item.description: Operation Metro Surge's impact in the Twin Cities can still be felt three months after it ended. Many immigrants are at risk of losing homes and businesses, and some are dealing with mental health challenges.
item.content:encoded:
Operation Metro Surge's impact in the Twin Cities can still be felt three months after it ended. Many immigrants are at risk of losing homes and businesses, and some are dealing with mental health challenges.

(Image credit: Tim Evans for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Primary elections in Ohio, Indiana offer latest temperature check on Trump popularity
item.description: The president seeks to oust Indiana Republicans who blocked a redistricting push in that state, while growing dissatisfaction with Trump's agenda gives Democrats an opening in Ohio.
item.content:encoded:
The president seeks to oust Indiana Republicans who blocked a redistricting push in that state, while growing dissatisfaction with Trump's agenda gives Democrats an opening in Ohio.

(Image credit: Jon Cherry)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: An explosion at a fireworks plant in China kills at least 26 people, state media says
item.description: Authorities said search and rescue at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has largely been completed, but verification of the casualties and identification of the victims are still underway.
item.content:encoded:
Authorities said search and rescue at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has largely been completed, but verification of the casualties and identification of the victims are still underway.

(Image credit: Chen Sihan/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
